title: Preesistenze medievali | Piazza dei Cavalieri
highlights: 1979, la tecnica d’indagine non invasiva della termografia, che rilevando la distribuzione del calore sulla superficie della facciata ha permesso di ottenere un’immagine ‘termica’ (non ottica) dei differenti materiali coperti dall’intonaco. Su questa base, nel 1987 Fabio Redi ha proposto una descrizione delle preesistenze medievali incorporate in facciata e una ricostruzione ipotetica dell’aspetto originario della struttura. Si trattava di un edificio in muratura piena di conci di pietra squadrata. La facciata, larga 14 m (e quindi 1,8 m in meno dell’attuale) era solcata al piano terreno da quattro arcate a sesto leggermente ribassato, di 2,5 m di luce,  ...    una struttura a parallelepipedo, a marcata orizzontalità: un edificio ‘a palazzotto’ e non a torre o casa-torre, come  ...    ugualmente in muratura piena, con loggia al piano terreno e polifore al piano nobile; e il palazzo arcivescovile, la  ...    congiunto al Palazzo, dove hora fanno residenza». title: Palazzo Vecchio History | Historical Overview of Florence's Icon
highlights: Located in the heart of Florence, Italy, the building was designed to serve as the seat of the city's government and the residence for the Signoria, Florence’s ruling body. The design includes elements of medieval fortress architecture like a massive, crenellated tower that symbolized the strength and independence of the  ...    The exterior of Palazzo Vecchio was built with pietra forte stone that enhanced its durability and defensive capabilities. Interior spaces were designed to accommodate governmental functions, with grand halls for assemblies and smaller rooms for administrative purposes. Over time, Palazzo Vecchio became a symbol of Florence's political life and  ...    Vasari created Salone dei Cinquecento, an expansive hall, adorned with grand frescoes depicting Florentine victories  ... title: A masterclass in architecture: Palazzo Vecchio of Florence
highlights:
Palazzo Vecchio is a grand testament to Medieval and Renaissance design. Once the seat of the Florentine government, this majestic palace is filled with historic architecture marked by perfectly symmetrical lines, stunning arches, and beautiful frescoes adorning the walls of its grand halls.  ...    - Architectural style: Gothic, Renaissance
- Area: Approximately 2.2 acres
- Founded: 1299  ...    The architectural style of Palazzo Vecchio in Florence is a stunning blend of Romanesque, Gothic, and Renaissance elements. The building was designed by some of the most renowned architects of its time, including Arnolfo di Cambio,  ...    The Palazzo's design is marked by its perfectly symmetrical lines and stunning arches. The attention to detail is  ...    can be seen in its large, pointed arches and high, vaulted ceilings.
